title: Arrays, Collections, Iterables, and Generators · Changed-Input Lab
summary: Transform ordered requests, unique identifiers, lookup tables, and streaming batches while preserving ownership and order. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a collection-processing and streaming pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose mutating or copying array operations and document ownership before updating shared data.
Use map, filter, reduce, find, some, every, flat, and flatMap from the required output and early-exit behavior.
Sort with a valid comparator, copying policy, stability expectations, and locale-aware text rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
